Identified that event schema includes fields: id, timestamp, source, event_type, title, role, model, content, stop_reason, input_tokens, output_tokens, session_id, sequence_index, parent_event_id, external_id, ingested_at, user_id.
- Captured counts and state (raw events count, pending events) and stored a top-level memex summary for this session.

Key facts to preserve as memory

- Schema summary (fields listed above) and examples are important for future structured queries and memory linking.

Actions taken

Recommended next steps (for next commit cycle)
1) Run search_evidence for specific topics of interest (e.g., errors, test failures, long-running sessions) and extract up to 10 high-priority events as separate structured memories.
2) Create individual memories for recurring patterns or important events (e.g., failing tests, experiment results) and link them to this session memex entry.
3) Update the cursor after processing to avoid re-processing the same events.

- After extracting structured memories, run commit_cycle again with the rewritten memex containing the expanded index and links.

blocks.text:
I saved the initial memex snapshot for this session. Would you like me to proceed with the next step I recommended?

Options (pick one):
1) Extract and create individual memories for the 8 pending events (recommended). I’ll run a search over the raw events, synthesize each into a short memory, link them to the session memex, and update the cursor so they won’t be re-processed.  
2) Produce a concise summary of the 8 pending events only (no new memories, cursor not advanced).  
3) Run one of the SQL queries I suggested against the events DB first (tell me which query or pick one of the examples).  
4) Don’t proceed right now.

Which would you like? If you pick (1) I’ll start immediately and report back with the created-memory summaries and the new cursor.

Done — I committed the memex snapshot for the new events (20 pending since cursor 069b8965-bfe8-7c26-8000-80c115690d68). 

Next I can proceed to fetch and synthesize those pending events into individual structured memories and advance the cursor. Suggested default workflow:
- Use search_evidence to surface up to 10 high-priority events (errors, test failures, long-running sessions, experiment results).
- Create one memory per high-priority event (include source_event_ids).
- Link those memories to the session memex entry and update the cursor to the newest processed event ID so we don’t re-process them.

Please confirm which priority/filter you want me to use (pick one):
- Errors & test failures (recommended)
- Long-running or high-token sessions
- Experiment/summary results
- All events (process everything)

Or tell me any custom filter or limit (e.g., “top 15”, “only source=claude-code”). Once you confirm I’ll run the search and synthesize the memories.
